free_text: The energy dependence of cross-sections of formation of

the reactions of protons with separated tin isotopes

Subent was superseded by O0988.
- By Compiler - Probably, cross section is cumulative,
because Y-86 production cross section is larger,
than Zr-86 production.
SD: SF4=Y-86g -> Y-86 in REACTION code.
- By Compiler - Y-85 (T1/2=2.7HR) has isomer state with
half-life 4.9 hr. The authors did not explain what
cross section has been measured.
- By Compiler - Probably, cross section is cumulative,
because Sr-85 production cross section is larger,
than Y-85 production.

than Sr-83 production.
- By Compiler - Rb-82m cannot be produced due to
radioactive decay of Sr-82.
- By Compiler - It is unclear, that Kr-76 deposit is
taken in account.
